text: I find myself amidst the constructed order of modernity. Bright lights and glass create a sense of transparency but also distance, as if everything is to be seen but not touched. The air feels heavy with the pressure to perform, to exhibit, to function. Am I merely a spectator here, or do I shape the meaning of this place by my presence? My reflection in the glass reminds me of the thin boundary between observer and observed. I am curious, slightly unsettled, and deeply aware of the artifice surrounding me.
